mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat, the discount-rate channel dominates and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next 20 trading days because expected financing costs and equity risk premiums ease.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods of non-increasing FEDFUNDS, or if the signal’s Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above recent levels raises real-rate expectations and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples compress.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average by at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with accompanying PAYEMS weakness signals deteriorating labor income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through weaker earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 pp over 3 months while PAYEMS growth turns negative over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined unemployment-and-payroll deterioration trigger does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained rises in RSAFS indicate stronger consumer demand and revenue momentum, which support equity earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher when RSAFS is trending up, or if the signal becomes negative in a rolling 2-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to weak CPIAUCSL growth indicates improving real output with contained price pressure, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days via stronger margins and lower stagflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is below its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-output/low-inflation condition fails to produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if reversing the CPI condition removes the effect entirely.

mechanism_narrative: A housing slowdown in HOUST acts as an early warning for softer construction-related demand and weaker cyclical earnings, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when housing starts fall sharply.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by non-negative 5-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the conditional mean return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a cooling labor regime, a higher UNRATE accompanied by lower PAYEMS reduces disposable income and raises recession odds, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-slowdown pattern does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns in rolling out-of-sample tests, or if each proxy alone explains all of the signal.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated, restrictive policy suppresses risk appetite and discount multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the high-rate high-inflation regime does not precede below-zero 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once either proxy is removed.
